Lau Kam Hung (劉金洪)

nationality.infer_reason: Born in Guangzhou and relocated to Hong Kong at age 21; nationality inferred from place of birth and relocation details.
nationality.source: The defendant was born in Guangzhou in July 1956 and is 68. He relocated to Hong Kong when he was 21.

age_at_offence.source:
Particulars of the offence are that the defendant on 2 April 2022, at room 2110, 21st floor, Hoi Yan House, Hoi Fu Court, No.2 Hoi Ting Road, Tai Kok Tsui, Kowloon, unlawfully trafficked in dangerous drugs... 

The defendant was born in Guangzhou in July 1956 and is 68.

age_at_sentencing.source: The defendant was born in Guangzhou in July 1956 and is 68.

gender.source: He relocated to Hong Kong when he was 21.

parental_status.source: The defendant contributes about RMB800 towards his daughter’s living expenses, who is 18 years old and studying nursing in Guangzhou.

health_conditions.name: Former drug abuser
health_conditions.source: At the time of sentencing, the applicant was a former drug abuser aged 68, with 20 previous convictions...

health_conditions.name: Cardiac disease, diabetes, kidney problems, hernia and polyps
health_conditions.source: The defendant suffers from cardiac disease, diabetes, kidney problems, hernia and polyps.

occupation.occupation_name: Former construction site worker; on CSSA since 2017
occupation.source: The defendant was working as a construction site worker in 2015, earning around $30,000, when he suffered a hand injury forcing him to stop. His health had since deteriorated and had to rely on CSSA of $5,300 per month for his daily and medical expenses since 2017.

monthly_wage.source: The defendant was working as a construction site worker in 2015, earning around $30,000, when he suffered a hand injury forcing him to stop. His health had since deteriorated and had to rely on CSSA of $5,300 per month for his daily and medical expenses since 2017.

government_subsidy_recipient.source: His health had since deteriorated and had to rely on CSSA of $5,300 per month for his daily and medical expenses since 2017.

criminal_records.source: The defendant has 19 convictions from 6 court appearances. He was convicted in 1979 for 7 charges of theft, together with one of rape and one of robbery, and sentenced to a total of 9 years imprisonment. His next convictions were for assault with intent to rob, indecent assault, and rape, and he was sentenced to a total of 12 years’ imprisonment. In 1996, he was convicted for managing a vice establishment and sentenced to 6 months’ imprisonment, suspended for 3 years. The next set of convictions were in July 2008 for assault occasioning actual bodily harm, criminal intimidation, and assaulting a police officer.
His last conviction was on 2 October 2019, when he was sentenced to 18 months’ imprisonment for possession of dangerous drugs.

positive_habits_after_arrest.source: He has since quit his drug use and plans to be reunited with his daughter in the mainland upon release, and to never return to Hong Kong.

family_supports.source: The defendant contributes about RMB800 towards his daughter’s living expenses, who is 18 years old and studying nursing in Guangzhou.
